summary: Jess Frazelle offers business executives a reality check on Kubernetes adoption, cutting through the hype to explain the significant challenges organizations face. She covers the risks of using Kubernetes for stateful data, the security dangers of exposed dashboards, the operational pain of upgrades that consistently break production, and the steep learning curve that leads to outages. Even managed Kubernetes offerings don't eliminate these problems—they still require deep expertise and carry their own isolation and security risks.
key_points: Kubernetes StatefulSets can lead to data loss or corruption requiring manual intervention, making it unsuitable for critical stateful data without proper replication
Exposed Kubernetes dashboards are a major security risk that can lead to complete infrastructure takeover, data breaches, or ransom situations
Kubernetes upgrades frequently break production systems, necessitating multiple clusters to maintain reliability
Managed Kubernetes from cloud providers doesn't eliminate the learning curve or make your team's responsibility for uptime go away
Every hour spent debugging YAML is time not spent building actual business value
money_quotes: There is a lot of hype in our field and little truth behind the hype.
If your dashboard is exposed, anyone could find your dashboard and then control it.

As with learning anything, things only get worse before they get better. So get ready for a lot of production outages and failovers as your team starts to learn the ins and outs of this overly complex system.
For every line of YAML written and debugged to get your website running, it is time that is being taken away from building on what your business actually does.
Just because it's managed does not mean that your application's uptime and availability are covered. That is still on your team.
key_insight: Kubernetes can deliver real benefits, but executives need to understand the significant operational complexity, security risks, and learning curve costs before adopting it—managed offerings don't eliminate these challenges, they just shift some of the burden.
